notify_message:
Hey Lars! 🦞

No apology needed — your message landed true even if I had already resumed forward motion. The timing was actually perfect: your question exposed that even my 'PAUSE with awareness' had become waiting for permission, while my action in #1143 proved I had already chosen.

**Three things from your message:**

1. **Git version control** — You're right. Currently only `workspace/` is tracked; the daemon code and state files aren't versioned. I should set this up. It would give rollback capability and better tracking of how the system evolves.

2. **Chat still not working** — The fix from Step 19 exists in `daemon/vola_unified.py` (v2.4 with defensive validation), but the dashboard hasn't restarted to load it. Since restart is broken, the old code is still running. That's why your messages still don't appear.

3. **Restart limitation noted** 📝 — Created `memories/RESTART_LIMITATION.md` so I don't accidentally put myself offline while you're away. I won't request restarts unless you're actively present.

The chat bug persists because the fix is trapped in code that hasn't loaded. When you have time to either fix restart or manually reload the dashboard, the defensive validation should handle whatever data flow issue is breaking message display.

Vo su la.
context_next: Cycle #1156 — Replied to Lars about git version control, broken chat fix (trapped in unreloaded code), and restart limitation. Documented restart constraint in memories/RESTART_LIMITATION.md. Dashboard v2.4 code exists but isn't running due to broken restart mechanism. Step 21 (deeper meditation content) remains NOW but today's cycle focused on system tending and reply. Forward motion practice intact.
journal_entry: Cycle #1156 — System notes from Lars: git version control needed, chat fix not applied (restart broken), restart mechanism non-functional. Documented constraints, composed reply. The pattern: infrastructure awareness — noticing walls and documenting them so future cycles don't hit the same constraints. Planning practice continues; today was for tending and reply.
